name: CONTRACT-BACKLOG-1
title: Prompt control: human interface to inspect and edit subagent context prompt before dispatch
description:
Every subagent call injects a constructed context prompt (policy docs, spec, tdd state, etc.). Currently this is opaque to the user — they cannot see what was assembled or correct it before dispatch.

Required capabilities:
1. INSPECT: Before dispatching a subagent, surface the full assembled prompt to the user in a readable form.
2. EDIT: Allow the user to modify the prompt (add, remove, or override sections) before it is sent.
3. BYPASS: The inspect/edit gate must be bypassable (one-time or session-long) for fully autonomous flows.

Implementation note (user suggestion): Model this as a contract — define a versioned schema for what constitutes a subagent prompt payload, so each injected section is a named, typed field. The human interface operates on the schema, not on raw text. This makes inspection and editing structured and auditable rather than free-form string manipulation.

Affected surfaces: contract (schema), tdd-state-machine (context block assembly), rabbit-cage (user-facing hook or skill for the inspect/edit UI).

history.note: Deferred: too large/complex for housekeeping waves. Requires dedicated design cycle. Filed for future roadmap consideration.
design completed in PR #127; reopening for implementation tracking (closed in error by long-running housekeeping session)
Deferred: requires dedicated design cycle. Large feature — human-editable subagent prompt interface needs architecture work beyond housekeeping scope.
design completed in PR #127; housekeeping closure was a context-drift error (originally filed 2026-05-15 well before housekeeping)

Size constraint observed during RABBIT-CAGE-BACKLOG-12 TDD cycle: dispatch-tdd-subagent.py assembled a ~90KB prompt (RABBIT-POLICY-BLOCK ~15KB inlining 3 policy files verbatim; rabbit-cage spec.md 70+ invariants ~50KB; TDD step instructions ~20KB; impl-suggestion ~5KB). Agent tool inline prompt has effective size limit making the assembled prompt unusable as a direct Agent call. Orchestrator had to construct an abbreviated ~5KB equivalent manually. Root cause: dispatch-tdd-subagent.py inlines full file content rather than referencing paths for lazy reading. Solution directions align with structured schema proposal here: (a) pass file paths not content for policy/spec, let subagent read at execution time; (b) define max prompt size invariant; (c) human inspect/edit interface (core of this backlog) becomes critical once prompt is referential not self-contained. Observed 2026-05-19 during BACKLOG-12 work.
Temporarily closed before CONTRACT-BACKLOG-19 housekeeping wave to prevent accidental closure by wave b/b filings; will be reopened after the wave completes

Implemented via 7-PR stack (#188-#194): A.1 schema+lint, A.2 assembler+hook source, A.3 runtime APIs+contract manifest, A.4 templates bundle, B tdd-subagent migration, C per-feature sweep (rabbit-feature/rabbit-file/rabbit-config), D dead-template cleanup. The prompt-contract machinery is now end-to-end: every registered skill/subagent has its invariable policy injections + variable slots declared in its owning feature.json; the contract-owned PreToolUse hook fires on Skill calls and the dispatch-tdd-subagent.py routes through build-prompt.py; stop-event runtime APIs sweep prompt files and surface injection failures.
